svg.title: Implemented RG residual error pathway for sparse single-winner classification
svg.desc: A 52-cell spiking neural circuit with nine retinal sources, a nine-cell L1 evidence sheet, a separate nine-cell residual sheet, eight L2 competitors, eight predictors, eight incumbent switch interneurons, and one shared L2 inhibitory relay. Representative indexed paths and counted bundles show all 274 directed projections.
svg.text: Residual/error pathway: full evidence + local incumbent release
Predictive inhibition is moved off L1E and onto a separate residual sheet; ordinary L2 WTA still selects exactly one category.
RG_RESIDUAL PRESET — separate from the legacy “rg” preset
RG → L1E → L2E keeps the complete input available for classification

g.text: edge detector broadcast; no inhibitory input

svg.text: ErrorE ≈ present evidence − predicted evidence (conductance-based cancellation, not engine arithmetic)

g.text: 8 Inhibitory (I) · one paired with each L2E
predicts features onto ErrorE—not onto L1E

svg.text: all ErrorE events broadcast to all SwitchI

g.text: 9 RG · 9 L1E · 9 ErrorE · 8 L2E · 8 PI · 8 SwitchI · 1 L2I
Every multiplicity appears beside its counted bundle.
No global NoveltyE cell; novelty stays distributed.
Reject repeated activity on either branch alone.
Never consult a global or sticky winner ID.
A. Familiar row {3,4,5}: stable single-owner classification
Prediction cancels residual activity without removing the complete L1 evidence.
• L1E sends the full row to all eight L2 competitors through the dense recognition path.
• L2E3’s real spike primes local trace x_3 and excites its paired PI3.
• Learned PI3 inhibition targets ErrorE3, ErrorE4, ErrorE5—not L1E3, L1E4, L1E5.
• With no residual broadcast, SwitchI3 stays silent and the incumbent remains the unique owner.
Stable classification is intended; initial smoke runs have not established it robustly across seeds.
B. New overlapping column {1,4,7}: release incumbent, then re-compete
The switch circuit removes only L2E3; it does not choose the rival.
• Can SwitchI implement strict temporal AND without either branch firing it alone?
• Does incumbent inhibition arrive before L2E3 contaminates its weights with novel features?
• After a rival wins, can its predictor learn the complete pattern quickly enough to silence residuals?
• Does the circuit settle to one stable final winner instead of oscillating between owners?
These remain open dynamical questions, not solved properties of the implementation.

svg.text: Representative indexed paths + counted fanout/fanin bundles are used in place of 274 overlapping individual lines.
